description: Refactor sequential and parallel-slot execution paths so they share prompt construction (three-builder model) and post-Claude processing (single iteration_pipeline module). Closes wave-mode gaps in learnings extraction, bandit feedback, PromptTooLong recovery, the already-complete fallback, and crash escalation.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` output
Rust: All scoped tests pass with `cargo test -p <crate>` for touched files
No breaking changes to public CLI surface or PRD JSON schema
All `&Connection` reads stay on the main thread (no rusqlite Send violations)
No `.unwrap()` in production paths unless provably safe and commented why
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — ~1 day now to save ~2+ weeks later (1:10+ ratio); we are pre-launch, foundations compound
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable, wired in per plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — qualityDimensions on every task tells you what 'good' looks like
Phase 2 foundation — prefer solutions that lay strong post-launch foundations (1:10+ savings ratio)
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding test
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at milestones — milestones must leave the trunk green including pre-existing failures
Ship working code with tests to prove it; handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Tests that mirror implementation internals (break when refactoring)
Abstractions with only one concrete use
Error messages that don't identify what went wrong
Catch-all error handlers that swallow context
String-sniffing for outcome dispatch where a structured discriminator is available (per learning #2005)
Reading &Connection on a slot worker thread — every DB read MUST stay on the main thread (rusqlite is !Send, learnings #1893/#1852/#1871)
Adding a non-Send field to SlotPromptBundle (e.g., Rc, RefCell) — guarded by compile-time assert
Behavior changes in Phase A — the prompt-builder split MUST be byte-identical for the snapshot fixture

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

acceptanceCriteria: Test file `tests/prompt_core.rs` (or `src/loop_engine/prompt/core.rs` test module) compiles
Happy-path test for format_task_json with task_id/title/files populated
Happy-path test for completion_instruction includes task_id and title
Edge-case test: build_learnings_block returns ('', vec![]) on retrieval error (e.g., missing FTS5 table on a fresh DB)
Edge-case test: build_source_context_block returns '' when project_root does not exist
Invariant test: format_task_json output parses back via serde_json::from_str (round-trip)
Known-bad discriminator: a stub that always returns '' for build_learnings_block fails the happy-path test (test must distinguish stub from real)
userStories.description: Write the initial tests for the new prompt::core module before it exists. Cover format_task_json, completion_instruction, build_learnings_block, build_source_context_block, build_tool_awareness_block, and build_key_decisions_block. Tests assert graceful degradation (empty string on error) and section content correctness.
edgeCases: build_learnings_block called against a fresh DB with no learnings — returns ('', vec![]) without panic
build_source_context_block called with project_root pointing to a nonexistent path — returns '' without panic
format_task_json called with a Task whose touchesFiles is empty — JSON output omits or empties files array, no crash
completion_instruction called with a non-code task (empty touchesFiles) — output includes the non-code-note from sequential's existing logic

failureModes.cause: Ollama down during embedding generation in build_learnings_block path
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Returns ('', vec![]) and logs eprintln warning; caller's prompt still builds

failureModes.cause: FTS5 table missing on fresh DB

invariants: format_task_json output is valid JSON parseable by serde_json::from_str
build_*_block helpers never panic on empty/missing inputs
userStories.notes: TDD: write tests first. These tests must reject a stub that returns empty strings unconditionally. Use real Task structs (per src/models/task.rs) — never hand-built JSON maps. Setup uses setup_db_with_migrations() per CLAUDE.md note about supersession-aware tests.
qualityDimensions: All `&Connection` reads stay on the main thread; helpers take &Connection by reference
No silent learning-injection failures: graceful degradation on retrieval errors
format_task_json output is round-trip-safe (parse → format → parse equality)
Helpers are infallible at the API boundary (return String / tuple, not Result)

acceptanceCriteria: Test asserts process_iteration_output extracts learnings from output via extract_learnings_from_output (count > 0 in `learnings` table after a fixture output containing `<learning>` tags)
Test asserts process_iteration_output records bandit feedback for shown_learning_ids (rows in `learning_feedback` with matching learning_id and task_id)

Test asserts skip_git_completion_detection=false attempts git detection (when in a repo)
Test asserts is_task_reported_already_complete fallback fires in BOTH wave (skip_git=true) and sequential (skip_git=false) modes
Test asserts ProcessingOutcome.tasks_completed dedups across multiple completion branches in one call (matches today's process_slot_result HashSet semantics)

userStories.description: Write the initial tests for the new shared post-Claude pipeline. Covers learnings extraction, feedback recording, status updates, completion detection (with skip-git flag), already-complete fallback, key-decisions extraction, deduped tasks_completed counter, and retroactive outcome mutation.
edgeCases: Slot 0 crashes pre-merge: outcome=Crash, shown_learning_ids=empty — process_iteration_output records feedback (no-op for empty ids), skips completion detection, still updates per-task crash map
<completed>X</completed> emitted by slot assigned task Y (cross-task completion) — Y is marked done; X stays in pending; both IDs appear in ProcessingOutcome.completed_task_ids
Tasks completed via different completion paths in one call (e.g., <task-status>:done AND <completed> tag for same id) — counted exactly once in tasks_completed
Output is empty string and claude_conversation is None — pipeline runs all branches without panic; no learnings extracted; no feedback recorded; outcome unchanged
Wave slot's claude_conversation is Some — extract_learnings_from_output prefers conversation over output (matches sequential preference at engine.rs:2034)

failureModes.expectedBehavior: eprintln warning; pipeline continues; ProcessingOutcome populated normally

failureModes.cause: DB UPDATE for status_updates fails partway
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Failure surfaces in ProcessingOutcome.status_updates_applied count; subsequent steps still run

invariants: Calling process_iteration_output once per Claude invocation produces the same DB state as today's inline sequential code
Calling process_iteration_output for N slots is equivalent to N independent sequential calls
Pipeline never propagates errors from best-effort observability writes (logged via eprintln!)
userStories.notes: These tests are the contract for FEAT-003. setup_db_with_migrations() required. Use real IterationOutcome and IterationContext structs. The mutation test must construct an Empty outcome and assert it mutates to Completed after pipeline call (matches sequential at engine.rs:3280, 3307, 3341, 3400, 3454).
qualityDimensions: Idempotent post-processing — exactly-once-per-task semantics match today's sequential behavior
No silent failure paths — best-effort writes log via eprintln!
Pipeline owns the dedup HashSet; callers consume ProcessingOutcome directly
claude_conversation preferred over output for learnings extraction (matches sequential)

acceptanceCriteria: Test asserts wave crash on task X populates ctx.crashed_last_iteration[X]=true

Wave crashes task X then wave picks X again → escalates (today: false negative when 'last slot' != X)
Sequential success on X → next pick of X does not escalate (map cleared)
Two crashes in a row on X → escalation fires both times via consecutive_failures path; per-task map does not double-escalate

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Test catches it: success-then-repick should NOT escalate

invariants: ctx.crashed_last_iteration[task_id] is the authoritative source for crash escalation
Map writes happen only on the main thread
userStories.notes: Use a synthetic IterationContext + repeated invocations rather than the real loop. Test must construct two consecutive iterations across paths (wave → sequential) and assert escalation fires on the second.
qualityDimensions: Crash escalation correctness via per-task HashMap
Map writes serialized on main thread (no Arc/Mutex needed)

acceptanceCriteria: Test asserts a slot at index 2 hitting PromptTooLong invokes overflow::handle_prompt_too_long with that slot's task_id
Test asserts model_overrides / overflow_recovered entries on IterationContext after the recovery are keyed on slot 2's task_id ONLY (slots 0/1/3 task_ids absent from those maps)

edgeCases: Same task ID hits PromptTooLong twice in two consecutive waves — ctx.overflow_recovered set tracks first-overflow only via .entry().or_insert_with()

Sequential follows a wave that recovered task X — sequential's PromptTooLong on X observes the existing override (continuity preserved)

failureModes.cause: Wave mode dispatcher forgets to call overflow handler
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Test catches it: model_overrides remains empty after a slot PromptTooLong

invariants: Per-slot overflow recovery is keyed on the slot's own task_id

userStories.notes: Use synthetic SlotResult + IterationContext rather than running real waves. Read overflow.rs:316-423 for handle_prompt_too_long invariants — order of operations is contractual: ctx update → DB UPDATE → stderr → dump → JSONL → rotate.
qualityDimensions: Per-slot overflow isolation: PromptTooLong on slot 2 does not corrupt slots 0/1/3
OverflowEvent.slot_index is additive serde change (existing consumers tolerate missing field)
Recovery state durable BEFORE best-effort observability writes (per CLAUDE.md overflow contract)

Use the Phase A snapshot to verify no prompt-side regression.
qualityDimensions: run_iteration shrinks to 'select task → spawn Claude → return raw IterationResult'

acceptanceCriteria: process_slot_result invokes iteration_pipeline::process_iteration_output with skip_git_completion_detection: true

Cross-task completion (slot processing X emits <completed>Y</completed>) preserved via ProcessingOutcome.completed_task_ids
Slot-prefixed logging (e.g., `[slot {idx}]`) preserved at the call site, not lifted into pipeline

process_slot_result passes slot_result.iteration_result.conversation.as_deref() into IterationProcessingParams.claude_conversation (so wave-mode learnings extraction prefers the structured conversation, matching sequential)
process_slot_result passes &slot_result.shown_learning_ids into IterationProcessingParams.shown_learning_ids (sourced from the bundle, threaded through the worker, no main-thread DB re-query)
Existing wave-mode tests pass after fixture updates

acceptanceCriteria: IterationContext (engine.rs:~224) gains `crashed_last_iteration: HashMap<String, bool>` field (or HashSet<String>)
iteration_pipeline::process_iteration_output writes ctx.crashed_last_iteration[task_id] = matches!(outcome, IterationOutcome::Crash(_))

consumers.mitigation: Add new field; remove legacy fields

semanticDistinctions.context: Wave crashes task X then sequential picks X
semanticDistinctions.currentBehavior: False negative: escalation does NOT fire (last_task_id is the wrong slot's id)
semanticDistinctions.requiredBehavior: Escalation fires (map[X]=true regardless of which path crashed it)

userStories.description: Replace IterationContext.last_task_id + last_was_crash with crashed_last_iteration: HashMap<String, bool>. Pipeline writes one entry per processed task. check_crash_escalation consults the map. Remove legacy fields.
userStories.notes: Read learnings #106, #1813, #213 for the legacy field semantics. After this task, learnings #106/#1813/#213 should be SUPERSEDED (use task-mgr learn --supersedes). The new field replaces the brittle 'last_task_id == current_task_id && last_was_crash' predicate (PRD §2.5 'Crash escalation correctness').
qualityDimensions: Crash escalation correctness via per-task map
Map writes are main-thread-only (no concurrency primitives needed)
Legacy fields fully removed once readers are audited
Map size bounded in practice by active task count
userStories.title: Phase C — Per-task crash tracking on IterationContext

TEST-INIT-005 tests pass (slot 2 PromptTooLong does not corrupt slots 0/1/3)
Existing sequential overflow tests pass unchanged

CONTRACT: order of operations preserved (ctx update → DB UPDATE → stderr → dump → JSONL → rotate)

consumers.mitigation: Audit confirmed safe under serial main-thread invocation

semanticDistinctions.requiredBehavior: Same four-rung ladder as sequential, isolated to the slot's own task_id
